title: CMMC AU.L2-3.3.2 — User-to-action traceability on CUI systems

compliance.assurance_rationale: SPL provides user-to-action traceability on CUI systems using Windows Security logon events — directly satisfying AU.L2-3.3.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that CMMC AU.L2-3.3.2 (Ensure unique user traceability) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.20.4: CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 4.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.20.4' running on WinEventLog:Security, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
spl:
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4624, 4648, 4672) earliest=-24h
| lookup cui_hosts.csv ComputerName AS dest OUTPUT cui_flag
| where cui_flag="true"
| eval user=coalesce(TargetUserName, SubjectUserName)
| eval logon_type=case(Logon_Type="2","Interactive", Logon_Type="3","Network", Logon_Type="10","RemoteInteractive", true(),Logon_Type)
| stats dc(dest) AS systems_accessed count AS actions values(logon_type) AS logon_types BY user
| where user!="-" AND user!="SYSTEM" AND user!="*$"
| sort - actions
| table user, systems_accessed, actions, logon_types
dataModelAcceleration: Enable acceleration for the Authentication data model (Authentication.Authentication).
description: Ensures unique user traceability on CUI-classified systems by correlating authentication events (logon, explicit credential use, privilege escalation) to individual user accounts. CMMC AU.L2-3.3.2 requires that actions can be traced to individual users so that they can be held accountable.
value: Maps every action on CUI systems to a named individual user, enabling the organisation to demonstrate that shared/generic accounts are not used for CUI access and that all activity is attributable.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for AU.L2-3.3.2 — User-to-action traceability on CUI systems. CMMC sets the bar for protecting sensitive information, and we keep evidence for the level we target.
implementation: (1) Verify data sources: `WinEventLog:Security`, `cui_hosts.csv` lookup; (2) Maintain CUI-scoped lookups; (3) Schedule search at compliance-aligned interval; (4) Route findings to ticketing queue; (5) Retain exports per records management policy.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security`, `cui_hosts.csv` lookup.
• Install and configure: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263).

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify that all required data sources are ingesting into Splunk. Maintain the CUI-scoped lookups (cui_hosts.csv, cui_network_segments.csv, etc.) with current asset inventory.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL (save as report or alert):

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4624, 4648, 4672) earliest=-24h
| lookup cui_hosts.csv ComputerName AS dest OUTPUT cui_flag
| where cui_flag="true"
| eval user=coalesce(TargetUserName, SubjectUserName)
| eval logon_type=case(Logon_Type="2","Interactive", Logon_Type="3","Network", Logon_Type="10","RemoteInteractive", true(),Logon_Type)
| stats dc(dest) AS systems_accessed count AS actions values(logon_type) AS logon_types BY user
| where user!="-" AND user!="SYSTEM" AND user!="*$"
| sort - actions
| table user, systems_accessed, actions, logon_types
```

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.
Step 4 — Operationalize
Schedule the search at an appropriate interval. Route findings to the compliance ticketing queue. Table of users with system count and action count. Bar chart of logon types per user. Single value for total unique users on CUI systems.
visualization: Table of users with system count and action count. Bar chart of logon types per user. Single value for total unique users on CUI systems.
knownFalsePositives: Level achievements take time during gap remediation, evidence packaging, or scheduled assessments.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src span=1d | sort - count
